description: Ancestral eating pattern focusing on whole, unprocessed foods — meats, fish, vegetables, fruits, nuts, and seeds

paleo_sweet_potato_hash.name: Sweet Potato Breakfast Hash
paleo_sweet_potato_hash.description: Hearty skillet hash with diced sweet potatoes, ground pork, bell peppers, and fresh herbs
ingredients: 1 medium sweet potato, peeled and diced (150g)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add diced sweet potato and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until fork-tender and lightly browned.
Push sweet potatoes to the side and add ground pork, breaking it apart. Cook for 5 minutes until no longer pink.
Add bell pepper, onion, paprika, and garlic powder. Cook for 3-4 minutes until vegetables soften.
Season with salt and pepper, toss everything together, and garnish with fresh parsley.

paleo_veggie_egg_scramble.name: Veggie-Loaded Egg Scramble
paleo_veggie_egg_scramble.description: Fluffy scrambled eggs packed with spinach, mushrooms, tomatoes, and fresh basil
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Melt ghee in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add sliced mushrooms and cook for 3 minutes until they release their moisture and turn golden.
Add spinach and diced tomato, stirring until spinach wilts, about 1 minute.
Whisk eggs with onion powder, salt, and pepper. Pour into the skillet and gently scramble with a spatula for 2-3 minutes until just set.
Remove from heat, top with torn basil, and serve immediately.

paleo_banana_pancakes.name: Banana Egg Pancakes
paleo_banana_pancakes.description: Simple two-ingredient pancakes made with ripe bananas and eggs, topped with fresh berries and walnuts
instructions: Mash bananas in a bowl until smooth. Whisk in eggs, cinnamon, and sea salt until well combined.
Heat coconut oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour batter in 3-inch rounds (about 2 tbsp each). Cook for 2 minutes until edges set and small bubbles form.
Flip gently and cook another 1-2 minutes until golden brown.
Stack pancakes and top with blueberries and chopped walnuts.

paleo_avocado_bacon_eggs.name: Avocado Bacon and Eggs
paleo_avocado_bacon_eggs.description: Crispy bacon strips alongside perfectly fried eggs served on a bed of sliced avocado
ingredients: 1/4 tsp everything bagel seasoning (no seed oils)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Place bacon strips in a cold skillet and cook over medium heat for 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway, until crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Pour off most of the bacon fat and add coconut oil to the skillet. Crack eggs into the pan and fry for 3-4 minutes until whites are set.
Arrange avocado slices on a plate, top with fried eggs and bacon strips.
Season with everything bagel seasoning, salt, and pepper.

paleo_sausage_frittata.name: Sausage and Pepper Frittata
paleo_sausage_frittata.description: Oven-baked frittata loaded with Italian sausage, roasted peppers, onions, and fresh oregano
ingredients: 6 oz Italian pork sausage (sugar-free, no additives)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Heat avocado oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat.
Remove sausage from casing and cook, crumbling with a spoon, for 5 minutes until browned. Add bell peppers and onion, cooking 3 more minutes.
Whisk eggs with oregano, salt, and pepper. Pour over the sausage and vegetables, gently tilting the pan to distribute evenly.
Transfer skillet to the oven and bake for 12-15 minutes until eggs are fully set and the top is lightly golden.
Let cool for 2 minutes, slice into wedges, and serve. Yields 2 servings.

paleo_coconut_chia_pudding.name: Coconut Chia Pudding
paleo_coconut_chia_pudding.description: Creamy overnight chia pudding made with full-fat coconut milk, topped with mango and toasted coconut flakes
instructions: In a jar or bowl, whisk together chia seeds, coconut milk, water, vanilla extract, and sea salt.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight, stirring once after the first 30 minutes to prevent clumping.
When ready to serve, stir the pudding and add a splash of coconut milk if too thick.
Top with diced mango and toasted coconut flakes.

paleo_smoked_salmon_plate.name: Smoked Salmon Breakfast Plate
paleo_smoked_salmon_plate.description: Wild-caught smoked salmon served with sliced cucumber, capers, red onion, and avocado
instructions: Arrange smoked salmon on one side of a plate.
Fan avocado slices and cucumber slices alongside the salmon.
Scatter capers and red onion rings over the plate.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice, then garnish with fresh dill.

paleo_turkey_breakfast_muffins.name: Turkey Sausage Egg Muffins
paleo_turkey_breakfast_muffins.description: Portable egg muffins baked with ground turkey, sun-dried tomatoes, and fresh spinach
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 6-cup muffin tin with coconut oil spray.
In a skillet over medium heat, brown the ground turkey for 5 minutes, breaking it into small pieces. Season with garlic powder and Italian seasoning.
Divide cooked turkey, chopped spinach, and sun-dried tomatoes evenly among the muffin cups.
Whisk eggs with salt and pepper, then pour evenly into each cup, filling about 3/4 full.
Bake for 18-20 minutes until eggs are set and tops are lightly golden. Cool 2 minutes before removing. Yields 6 muffins (3 per serving).

paleo_plantain_egg_bowl.name: Fried Plantain Egg Bowl
paleo_plantain_egg_bowl.description: Pan-fried sweet plantains with scrambled eggs, black olives, and a drizzle of chimichurri
ingredients: 1 ripe plantain, peeled and sliced on the bias
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add plantain slices and fry 2-3 minutes per side until golden and caramelized. Transfer to a plate.
Add remaining coconut oil to the skillet. Scramble eggs over medium-low heat for 2-3 minutes until just set.
Arrange fried plantains and scrambled eggs in a bowl. Top with sliced olives.
Drizzle chimichurri over the top and sprinkle with salt.

paleo_berry_smoothie_bowl.name: Mixed Berry Smoothie Bowl
paleo_berry_smoothie_bowl.description: Thick blended bowl of mixed berries and coconut cream, topped with sliced almonds, hemp seeds, and fresh fruit
ingredients: 1 cup frozen mixed berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries)
instructions: Blend frozen mixed berries, frozen banana, coconut cream, and 2 tbsp water in a high-speed blender until thick and smooth. The consistency should be thicker than a smoothie.
Pour into a bowl and smooth the top with a spoon.
Top with almond butter, sliced almonds, hemp seeds, and fresh strawberry slices.
Serve immediately before it melts.

paleo_zucchini_hash_browns.name: Zucchini Hash Browns with Eggs
paleo_zucchini_hash_browns.description: Crispy shredded zucchini patties pan-fried in ghee, served with two sunny-side-up eggs
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Place shredded zucchini in a clean kitchen towel and squeeze out as much moisture as possible.
Toss zucchini with tapioca starch, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Form into 4 small patties.
Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook patties 3-4 minutes per side until crispy and golden.
In a separate pan, heat remaining ghee and fry eggs sunny-side-up for 3 minutes.
Plate hash brown patties topped with the eggs. Season with additional salt and pepper.

paleo_pork_apple_patties.name: Pork and Apple Breakfast Patties
paleo_pork_apple_patties.description: Savory-sweet breakfast sausage patties made with ground pork, grated apple, sage, and a touch of maple
ingredients: 1/2 Granny Smith apple, peeled and finely grated
instructions: In a bowl, combine ground pork, grated apple, sage, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Mix gently until just combined.
Form mixture into 4 patties, about 1/2 inch thick.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook patties for 4-5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through (internal temp 160°F).
Rest for 1 minute before serving. Pair with fresh fruit or a side of greens.

paleo_grilled_chicken_salad.name: Grilled Chicken Power Salad
paleo_grilled_chicken_salad.description: Herb-marinated grilled chicken over mixed greens with avocado, cherry tomatoes, pecans, and lemon vinaigrette
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Marinate chicken breast with avocado oil, Italian herbs, salt, and pepper for at least 10 minutes.
Grill chicken over medium-high heat for 6-7 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 165°F. Let rest 5 minutes, then slice.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate. Top with sliced chicken, diced avocado, cherry tomatoes, and pecans.
Whisk olive oil and lemon juice together with a pinch of salt. Drizzle over the salad.

paleo_tuna_stuffed_avocado.name: Tuna-Stuffed Avocados
paleo_tuna_stuffed_avocado.description: Ripe avocado halves filled with wild-caught tuna mixed with celery, red onion, and a tangy lemon-olive oil dressing
ingredients: 1 can (5 oz) wild-caught albacore tuna, drained
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In a bowl, flake the drained tuna with a fork. Add diced celery and red onion.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, and Dijon mustard. Pour over tuna mixture and toss to combine.
Season with salt and pepper. Spoon the tuna salad into each avocado half.
Sprinkle with paprika and serve immediately.

paleo_lettuce_wrap_burgers.name: Lettuce Wrap Burgers
paleo_lettuce_wrap_burgers.description: Juicy grass-fed beef patties wrapped in crisp butter lettuce with tomato, pickles, and special sauce
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season ground beef with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Form into 2 patties about 3/4 inch thick.
Cook patties in a skillet over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes per side for medium doneness.
Mix paleo mayo and mustard together for the special sauce.
Stack two lettuce leaves for each burger. Place a patty on each, top with tomato, pickles, onion rings, and special sauce.
Wrap lettuce around the burger and secure with a toothpick if needed.

paleo_shrimp_cauliflower_rice.name: Garlic Shrimp Cauliflower Rice Bowl
paleo_shrimp_cauliflower_rice.description: Sauteed garlic shrimp served over seasoned cauliflower rice with snap peas, carrots, and coconut aminos
ingredients: 6 oz large shrimp, peeled and deveined
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat. Add shrimp and cook 2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add garlic and ginger. Stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add cauliflower rice, snap peas, and carrots. Cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add coconut aminos and sesame oil. Toss to combine, then return shrimp to the pan.
Cook another minute to heat through. Season with salt and serve in bowls.

paleo_chicken_zoodle_soup.name: Chicken Zoodle Soup
paleo_chicken_zoodle_soup.description: Comforting chicken soup made with zucchini noodles, carrots, celery, and fresh herbs in a savory bone broth
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced chicken and cook 5 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, saute onion, carrot, and celery for 4 minutes until softened.
Pour in bone broth, add bay leaf and thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer for 12 minutes.
Return chicken to the pot. Add spiralized zucchini noodles and cook for 2-3 minutes until just tender.
Remove bay leaf, season with salt and pepper, and ladle into bowls.

paleo_thai_chicken_salad.name: Thai Chicken Salad
paleo_thai_chicken_salad.description: Shredded chicken tossed with crunchy cabbage, mango, cilantro, and cashews in a zesty lime-coconut dressing
instructions: In a large bowl, combine both cabbages, shredded chicken, mango, and cilantro.
Whisk together coconut aminos, lime juice, almond butter, and sesame oil until smooth to make the dressing.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss thoroughly to coat.
Top with roasted cashews and serve.

paleo_steak_sweet_potato_bowl.name: Steak and Sweet Potato Bowl
paleo_steak_sweet_potato_bowl.description: Sliced grass-fed sirloin over roasted sweet potato cubes with arugula, pickled onions, and chimichurri
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss sweet potato cubes with half the avocado oil, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Roast for 20-22 minutes until tender and caramelized.
Season steak with salt and pepper. Heat remaining avocado oil in a skillet over high heat. Sear steak 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes, then slice against the grain.
Arrange arugula in a bowl. Top with roasted sweet potato and sliced steak.
Drizzle with chimichurri and garnish with pickled red onion.

paleo_turkey_collard_wraps.name: Turkey Collard Green Wraps
paleo_turkey_collard_wraps.description: Sliced turkey breast rolled in blanched collard greens with avocado, bacon, and sprouts
ingredients: 2 large collard green leaves, stems trimmed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Blanch collard leaves in boiling water for 30 seconds, then immediately transfer to ice water. Pat completely dry.
Lay collard leaves flat. Shave down the thick center stem with a knife so the leaves roll easily.
Spread mashed avocado and Dijon mustard over each leaf. Layer turkey slices, bacon, and sprouts.
Roll tightly like a burrito, tucking in the sides. Slice in half diagonally and serve.

paleo_blt_sweet_potato_toast.name: BLT Sweet Potato Toast
paleo_blt_sweet_potato_toast.description: Thick slices of roasted sweet potato topped with bacon, lettuce, tomato, and avocado mayo
ingredients: 1 large sweet potato, sliced lengthwise into 1/2-inch planks
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Brush sweet potato planks with avocado oil, season with salt, and bake 20-22 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender with crisp edges.
Meanwhile, cook bacon in a skillet until crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Spread paleo mayo on each sweet potato toast.
Layer lettuce, tomato slices, and bacon on top. Season with pepper and serve open-faced.

paleo_salmon_avocado_bowl.name: Salmon Poke-Style Bowl
paleo_salmon_avocado_bowl.description: Cubed fresh salmon tossed in coconut aminos with cucumber, avocado, pickled ginger, and sesame over cauliflower rice
instructions: Saute cauliflower rice in avocado oil over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until tender. Transfer to a bowl.
Toss cubed salmon with coconut aminos and sesame oil. Let marinate for 5 minutes.
Arrange marinated salmon, avocado slices, diced cucumber, and pickled ginger over the cauliflower rice.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve immediately.

paleo_egg_drop_soup.name: Paleo Egg Drop Soup
paleo_egg_drop_soup.description: Silky bone broth egg drop soup with shiitake mushrooms, bok choy, and green onions
ingredients: 1 tsp tapioca starch mixed with 1 tbsp water
Salt and white pepper to taste
instructions: Bring bone broth to a boil in a pot. Add mushrooms, ginger, and coconut aminos. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add bok choy and cook 2 minutes until wilted. Stir in the tapioca starch slurry to slightly thicken the broth.
Beat eggs in a small bowl. While stirring the soup in a circular motion, slowly drizzle in the beaten eggs to form silky ribbons.
Remove from heat immediately. Season with salt and white pepper, garnish with sliced green onions.

paleo_mediterranean_plate.name: Mediterranean Lamb Plate
paleo_mediterranean_plate.description: Seasoned ground lamb over roasted vegetables with olives, fresh mint, and a tahini-lemon drizzle
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook ground lamb with cumin and coriander for 6-7 minutes until browned. Drain any excess fat.
In the same pan, add zucchini slices and cook 4-5 minutes until tender with slight char. Add cherry tomatoes for the last minute.
Arrange vegetables on a plate, top with seasoned lamb and olives.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 1 tbsp water. Drizzle over the plate and garnish with fresh mint.

paleo_herb_roasted_salmon.name: Herb-Crusted Roasted Salmon
paleo_herb_roasted_salmon.description: Wild-caught salmon fillet with a fresh herb crust, served alongside roasted asparagus and lemon wedges
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mix parsley, dill, garlic, 1 tbsp avocado oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper into a paste. Press the herb mixture onto the top of the salmon fillet.
Toss asparagus with remaining avocado oil, salt, and pepper. Arrange around the salmon on the baking sheet.
Roast for 15-18 minutes until salmon flakes easily and asparagus is tender-crisp.
Serve with fresh lemon wedges.

paleo_chicken_thigh_sweet_potato.name: Crispy Chicken Thighs with Mashed Sweet Potato
paleo_chicken_thigh_sweet_potato.description: Pan-seared chicken thighs with crispy skin over silky coconut mashed sweet potatoes and sauteed green beans
ingredients: 2 bone-in skin-on chicken thighs (about 8 oz total)
1 large sweet potato, peeled and cubed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Pat chicken thighs very dry with paper towels. Season generously with garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.

Heat avocado oil in a cast iron skillet over medium heat. Place chicken skin-side down and cook for 12-15 minutes without moving until skin is deeply golden and crispy. Flip and cook 10 more minutes until cooked through (165°F).
Meanwhile, boil sweet potato cubes in salted water for 12-15 minutes until fork tender. Drain and mash with coconut milk, half the ghee, salt, and pepper.
In a separate pan, saute green beans in remaining ghee for 4-5 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Plate mashed sweet potato, top with chicken thighs, and serve green beans alongside.

paleo_beef_stir_fry.name: Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry
paleo_beef_stir_fry.description: Tender strips of flank steak stir-fried with broccoli, bell peppers, and water chestnuts in a savory coconut aminos sauce
ingredients: 6 oz flank steak, sliced thin against the grain
instructions: Toss sliced steak with 1 tbsp coconut aminos and tapioca starch. Let sit 10 minutes.
Heat avocado oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat until smoking. Sear steak strips for 2 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add broccoli, bell pepper, and water chestnuts to the wok. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cooking 30 seconds more.
Return beef to the wok. Add remaining coconut aminos and sesame oil. Toss everything together for 1 minute.
Serve immediately over cauliflower rice if desired.

paleo_stuffed_bell_peppers.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers
paleo_stuffed_bell_peppers.description: Colorful bell peppers filled with seasoned ground beef, cauliflower rice, tomatoes, and fresh herbs, baked until tender
ingredients: 2 large bell peppers (any color), halved and seeded
1/2 cup diced tomatoes (canned, no sugar added)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Place pepper halves cut-side up in a baking dish.
Heat avocado oil in a skillet. Cook ground beef and onion for 6 minutes until browned. Add garlic, cumin, and chili powder, cooking 1 more minute.
Stir in cauliflower rice and diced tomatoes. Cook 3 minutes until cauliflower is tender. Season with salt and pepper.
Divide the filling among the pepper halves, packing it in generously.
Cover with foil and bake 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake 5 more minutes. Garnish with cilantro.

paleo_lemon_garlic_shrimp.name: Lemon Garlic Shrimp with Zoodles
paleo_lemon_garlic_shrimp.description: Succulent shrimp sauteed in garlic and lemon butter served over spiralized zucchini noodles with cherry tomatoes
ingredients: 8 oz large shrimp, peeled and deveined
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add shrimp in a single layer and cook 2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining ghee, garlic, and red pepper flakes. Cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add zucchini noodles and cherry tomatoes. Toss for 2-3 minutes until zoodles are just tender.
Return shrimp to the pan, squeeze lemon juice over everything, and toss to combine.
Plate and garnish with fresh parsley.

paleo_pork_chops_applesauce.name: Pan-Seared Pork Chops with Cinnamon Applesauce
paleo_pork_chops_applesauce.description: Thick-cut bone-in pork chops seared to golden perfection, served with homemade chunky cinnamon applesauce and roasted Brussels sprouts
ingredients: 2 medium apples, peeled, cored, and diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss Brussels sprouts with 1 tbsp avocado oil, salt, and pepper. Roast for 20-25 minutes until crispy.
Meanwhile, simmer diced apples with water, cinnamon, and nutmeg in a small saucepan over medium-low heat for 15 minutes, mashing lightly with a fork for a chunky sauce.
Pat pork chop dry and season with rosemary, salt, and pepper. Heat remaining avocado oil in an oven-safe skillet over high heat.
Sear pork chop 3 minutes per side until golden crust forms. Transfer skillet to oven for 8-10 minutes until internal temp reaches 145°F. Rest 5 minutes.
Serve pork chop with applesauce and roasted Brussels sprouts.

paleo_coconut_curry_chicken.name: Coconut Curry Chicken
paleo_coconut_curry_chicken.description: Tender chicken simmered in a rich coconut milk curry with sweet potatoes, spinach, and warming spices
ingredients: 6 oz boneless skinless chicken thighs, cubed
1 small sweet potato, peeled and cubed (120g)
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat. Saute onion for 3 minutes, then add garlic, curry powder, turmeric, and ginger. Stir 1 minute until fragrant.
Add cubed chicken and cook 4 minutes until lightly browned on all sides.
Pour in coconut milk and add sweet potato cubes. Bring to a simmer and cook covered for 18-20 minutes until sweet potato is tender and chicken is cooked through.
Stir in spinach and cook 2 minutes until wilted. Season with salt.
Serve in bowls, optionally over cauliflower rice.

paleo_balsamic_glazed_steak.name: Balsamic Glazed Steak with Roasted Vegetables
paleo_balsamic_glazed_steak.description: Grass-fed ribeye steak with a balsamic reduction, accompanied by roasted root vegetables and fresh arugula
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss beet, carrot, and parsnips with 1 tbsp avocado oil, thyme, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 22-25 minutes until tender.
Simmer balsamic vinegar in a small saucepan over medium heat for 5-6 minutes until reduced by half to a glaze consistency.
Pat steak dry and season generously. Heat remaining avocado oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat. Sear 4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes.
Slice steak against the grain. Arrange over arugula with roasted vegetables.
Drizzle balsamic glaze over the steak and vegetables.

paleo_turkey_meatballs_marinara.name: Turkey Meatballs with Roasted Tomato Marinara
paleo_turkey_meatballs_marinara.description: Baked turkey meatballs in a slow-roasted cherry tomato marinara, served over spaghetti squash
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Halve spaghetti squash, scoop seeds, brush with 1/2 tbsp avocado oil, and roast cut-side down for 30-35 minutes until tender.
Mix ground turkey, egg, almond flour, Italian seasoning, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Form into 12 small meatballs.
Heat 1 tbsp avocado oil in an oven-safe skillet. Brown meatballs on all sides for 4-5 minutes. Add cherry tomatoes, garlic, and remaining avocado oil around the meatballs.
Transfer skillet to the oven and bake 15 minutes. Tomatoes will burst and form a sauce. Smash them lightly with a fork.
Scrape spaghetti squash strands with a fork. Serve meatballs and marinara over the squash, garnished with fresh basil.

paleo_grilled_mahi_tacos.name: Grilled Mahi Mahi Tacos
paleo_grilled_mahi_tacos.description: Blackened mahi mahi in jicama shell tacos with mango salsa, shredded cabbage, and creamy avocado crema
ingredients: 4 round jicama slices (1/4-inch thick, as taco shells)
instructions: Rub mahi mahi with blackening seasoning and avocado oil. Grill or pan-sear over high heat for 4-5 minutes per side until cooked through and flaky. Break into chunks.
Make mango salsa by combining diced mango, red onion, cilantro, and half the lime juice.
Blend avocado with remaining lime juice and 1 tbsp water for the crema.
Assemble tacos: place jicama rounds on a plate, top each with shredded cabbage, mahi mahi chunks, mango salsa, and a drizzle of avocado crema.

paleo_slow_cooker_pot_roast.name: Slow Cooker Pot Roast
paleo_slow_cooker_pot_roast.description: Fork-tender grass-fed chuck roast slow-cooked with carrots, parsnips, and onions in an herb-infused bone broth
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season chuck roast generously with salt and pepper. Heat avocado oil in a skillet over high heat and sear roast 3 minutes per side until deeply browned.
Place onion quarters, carrots, parsnips, and garlic in the bottom of a slow cooker.
Set the seared roast on top of the vegetables. Add bone broth, rosemary, and thyme.
Cook on low for 7-8 hours until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Remove herb sprigs. Serve roast with vegetables and cooking liquid spooned over the top.

paleo_tuscan_chicken.name: Tuscan Chicken with Sun-Dried Tomatoes
paleo_tuscan_chicken.description: Pan-seared chicken breasts in a creamy coconut milk sauce with sun-dried tomatoes, spinach, and garlic
ingredients: 1/4 cup sun-dried tomatoes (oil-packed), drained and sliced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season chicken with salt, pepper, basil, and oregano. Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 5-6 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F). Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, saute garlic for 30 seconds. Add sun-dried tomatoes and cook 1 minute.
Pour in coconut milk and nutritional yeast, stirring until combined. Simmer 3-4 minutes until slightly thickened.
Add spinach and stir until wilted. Return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over it.
Serve with the creamy Tuscan sauce ladled generously on top.

paleo_lamb_burger_patties.name: Herb-Spiced Lamb Burgers with Caramelized Onions
paleo_lamb_burger_patties.description: Seasoned lamb patties topped with sweet caramelized onions, arugula, and a zesty lemon-tahini sauce
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat ghee in a skillet over medium-low heat. Add sliced onions with a pinch of salt. Cook slowly for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until deeply golden and caramelized.
Meanwhile, mix ground lamb with cumin, coriander, cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Form into 2 patties.
Heat avocado oil in a separate skillet over medium-high heat. Cook lamb patties 4-5 minutes per side for medium.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 1 tbsp water for the sauce.
Plate lamb patties on a bed of arugula, top with caramelized onions, and drizzle with lemon-tahini sauce.

paleo_sheet_pan_sausage_veggies.name: Sheet Pan Sausage and Vegetables
paleo_sheet_pan_sausage_veggies.description: Italian sausages roasted alongside sweet potatoes, Brussels sprouts, and red onion with rosemary and garlic
ingredients: 2 links Italian pork sausage (sugar-free, about 6 oz)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Spread sweet potato cubes, Brussels sprouts, red onion wedges, and garlic on the sheet pan. Drizzle with avocado oil, season with rosemary, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat.
Nestle sausage links among the vegetables.
Roast for 28-30 minutes, flipping sausages halfway through, until sausages are cooked (160°F) and vegetables are golden and tender.
Let rest 3 minutes, slice sausages, and serve everything together.

paleo_guacamole_veggie_sticks.name: Classic Guacamole with Veggie Sticks
paleo_guacamole_veggie_sticks.description: Chunky fresh guacamole with lime and cilantro, served with crisp jicama sticks, bell pepper strips, and cucumber rounds
ingredients: 1/2 red bell pepper, cut into strips
instructions: Halve the avocado and scoop flesh into a bowl. Mash with a fork to desired consistency (leave it chunky).
Fold in red onion, cilantro, jalapeño, and lime juice. Season with salt.
Arrange jicama sticks, bell pepper strips, and cucumber rounds on a plate alongside the guacamole.
Serve immediately for best flavor and color.

paleo_trail_mix.name: Paleo Trail Mix
paleo_trail_mix.description: Crunchy homemade trail mix with raw almonds, walnuts, pumpkin seeds, coconut chips, and dark chocolate chunks
ingredients: 1 tbsp dark chocolate chips (85%+ cacao, dairy-free)
1 tbsp dried cranberries (no sugar added, apple juice sweetened)
instructions: Combine all nuts, seeds, coconut chips, chocolate chips, and cranberries in a small bowl or jar.
Toss gently to mix evenly.
Portion into a small container or snack bag. Keeps well for up to 1 week at room temperature.

paleo_hard_boiled_eggs_everything.name: Everything Seasoned Hard-Boiled Eggs
paleo_hard_boiled_eggs_everything.description: Perfectly cooked hard-boiled eggs sprinkled with everything bagel seasoning and served with cherry tomatoes
instructions: Place eggs in a saucepan and cover with cold water by 1 inch. Bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
Remove from heat, cover with a lid, and let sit for exactly 10 minutes.
Transfer eggs to an ice bath for 3 minutes, then peel.
Slice eggs in half, sprinkle with everything bagel seasoning and flaky salt. Serve with cherry tomatoes.

paleo_apple_almond_butter.name: Apple Slices with Almond Butter
paleo_apple_almond_butter.description: Crisp apple slices paired with creamy almond butter and a sprinkle of cinnamon and hemp seeds
ingredients: 1 medium apple (Honeycrisp or Fuji), cored and sliced
2 tbsp almond butter (no added sugar or oils)
instructions: Core the apple and slice into wedges.
Arrange apple slices on a plate. Drizzle or dollop almond butter alongside for dipping.
Sprinkle hemp seeds and cinnamon over the almond butter.

paleo_coconut_energy_balls.name: Coconut Date Energy Balls
paleo_coconut_energy_balls.description: No-bake energy balls made with Medjool dates, almond butter, coconut flakes, and a touch of vanilla
instructions: Add dates, almond butter, 2 tbsp shredded coconut, coconut oil, vanilla, and salt to a food processor. Pulse until a sticky dough forms.
Roll mixture into 6 small balls (about 1 tablespoon each).
Roll balls in the remaining shredded coconut to coat.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up. Store in the fridge for up to 1 week. Makes 6 balls (3 per serving).

paleo_beef_jerky_homemade.name: Homemade Beef Jerky
paleo_beef_jerky_homemade.description: Thinly sliced grass-fed beef marinated in coconut aminos, garlic, and smoked paprika, then oven-dried until chewy
ingredients: 6 oz grass-fed flank steak, sliced very thin against the grain
instructions: Freeze steak for 30 minutes to make slicing easier. Slice into 1/8-inch thin strips against the grain.
Combine coconut aminos, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, pepper, and ginger in a bowl. Add beef strips and toss to coat. Marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight in the fridge.
Preheat oven to 175°F. Line baking sheets with parchment and place a wire rack on top. Lay beef strips in a single layer.
Bake for 3.5-4 hours, flipping halfway, until jerky is dry but still pliable.
Cool completely before storing in an airtight container. Keeps for 1-2 weeks. Serves 2.

paleo_sweet_potato_chips.name: Baked Sweet Potato Chips
paleo_sweet_potato_chips.description: Paper-thin sweet potato rounds baked until perfectly crispy, seasoned with sea salt and rosemary
ingredients: 1 large sweet potato, sliced paper-thin (use a mandoline)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
Toss sweet potato slices with avocado oil until evenly coated.
Arrange slices in a single layer on baking sheets (do not overlap).
Bake for 10 minutes, flip each chip, then bake another 10-12 minutes, watching closely to prevent burning. Some chips may finish before others — remove those early.
Immediately sprinkle with rosemary and sea salt while still hot. Chips will continue to crisp as they cool.

paleo_prosciutto_wrapped_dates.name: Prosciutto-Wrapped Dates with Pecans
paleo_prosciutto_wrapped_dates.description: Sweet Medjool dates stuffed with toasted pecans and wrapped in salty prosciutto, then baked until crispy
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Stuff each pitted date with a toasted pecan half.
Wrap each stuffed date tightly with a half-slice of prosciutto.
Place seam-side down on the baking sheet. Bake for 10-12 minutes until prosciutto is crispy.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt and let cool 2 minutes before serving. Makes 6 pieces (3 per serving).

paleo_cucumber_salmon_bites.name: Cucumber Smoked Salmon Bites
paleo_cucumber_salmon_bites.description: Thick cucumber rounds topped with smoked salmon, avocado cream, capers, and fresh dill
ingredients: 1 English cucumber, cut into 1/2-inch rounds (about 12 rounds)
instructions: Slice cucumber into thick rounds and arrange on a plate or platter.
Mix mashed avocado with lemon juice and a pinch of pepper for the cream.
Spread a small amount of avocado cream on each cucumber round.
Top with a piece of smoked salmon, a few capers, and a sprig of dill.

paleo_plantain_chips_salsa.name: Crispy Plantain Chips with Fresh Salsa
paleo_plantain_chips_salsa.description: Thin-sliced green plantain chips baked until golden, served with a bright fresh tomato and jalapeño salsa
ingredients: 1 green plantain, peeled and thinly sliced
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Toss plantain slices with melted coconut oil and salt.
Spread in a single layer on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake 8 minutes, flip, and bake another 8-10 minutes until golden and crispy.
While chips bake, combine diced tomatoes, red onion, cilantro, jalapeño, lime juice, and salt for the fresh salsa.
Serve warm plantain chips with the salsa on the side.

paleo_chicken_avocado_wrap.name: Chicken Avocado Lettuce Wraps
paleo_chicken_avocado_wrap.description: Seasoned shredded chicken with creamy avocado, pico de gallo, and lime crema wrapped in crisp romaine leaves
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Toss shredded chicken with cumin, half the lime juice, salt, and pepper.
Mix paleo mayo with remaining lime juice for the crema.
Combine diced tomato, red onion, and cilantro for a quick pico de gallo.
Divide chicken and avocado slices among the romaine leaves. Top with pico and drizzle with lime crema.
Fold and eat like tacos.

paleo_cauliflower_chili.name: Smoky Beef and Sweet Potato Chili
paleo_cauliflower_chili.description: Hearty no-bean chili with grass-fed ground beef, sweet potato chunks, fire-roasted tomatoes, and smoky spices
ingredients: 1 small sweet potato, peeled and diced (120g)
1/2 cup fire-roasted diced tomatoes (canned, no sugar added)
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a pot over medium-high heat. Brown ground beef with onion for 5-6 minutes, breaking into pieces. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic, chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika. Stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced sweet potato, fire-roasted tomatoes, and bone broth. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes until sweet potato is tender.
Season with salt and pepper. Ladle into a bowl and garnish with sliced green onion.
